key_insight: FEVessel learns a mesh-independent 3D pressure-vessel surrogate without FEM labels, then uses its prediction to accelerate iterative finite element solves.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical way to analyze pressure vessels across changing materials, geometries, loads, and even degenerate meshes while reducing solve time and manual mesh repair.
method: Represents each vessel as a point cloud with coordinate, material, and load features.
Pretrains a Transolver operator on total potential energy instead of supervised FEM displacement labels.
Uses the pretrained prediction as a warm start for algebraic multigrid/iterative FEM solvers.
evidence_signal: The model reports 1.35% relative displacement error, 2.07% strain error, a drop from 195 to 18 AMG iterations, and a 9.2× wall-clock speedup at 10^-3 tolerance, while staying below 3.66% error on inverted/sliver meshes.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the energy-based pretraining and point-cloud representation, so transfer to new vessel families or extreme loading regimes may still need careful validation.
paper_title: FEVessel: Mesh-Independent Analysis of 3D Pressure Vessels with the Label-Free Pretrained Finite Element Method
